closureNotes.outcome:
Deploy-dev lane landed on EB main at 5817cc4 (PR #311). Per v1.0.0 spec (SR PR #142 ad6630d): 9 files landed (8 declared + 1 substrate-discovered patterns.yml entry — see overrideRecord). NEW (4): .github/workflows/build-dev-images.yml, .github/workflows/deploy-dev.yml, .github/labels.yml, .dlp/deliverables/d-2026-06-06-eb-deploy-dev-via-pr-label/SPEC.md. MOD (5): infrastructure/terraform/environments/development/variables.tf (image_tag var declared), infrastructure/terraform/environments/development/main.tf (IMAGE_TAG line in local_file.development_env), docker-compose.development.yml (3 hardcoded SHAs → ${IMAGE_TAG:-latest}), .roadmap/decision-log.json (DEC d-2026-06-06-dev-deploy-pull-model-tf-apply-not-watchtower appended), infrastructure/workflows/patterns.yml (entries added at execution for both new workflows). All required CI checks green. NO Watchtower on dev (TF apply pull doctrine per DEC entry). PR label deploy-to-dev becomes the L4 named-step gate for dev deploys. Open watchtower-on-dev question from LUHTECH-PLATFORM-CLOSURE-STRATEGY-2026-04-27.md §5 resolved.

AMENDMENT NOTE (v1.0.1): This TRUTH-DELIVERED was originally landed at SR main 762fc42 (PR #143) by EB-chat in direct violation of memory #10 (SR write authority), memory #21 (EB CLI in SR repo), and memory #24 (cross-repo routing). Original content (SHA e8ada083) misclassified the patterns.yml authoring gap as debt rather than substrate-correction, omitted rulePipelineEvidence entirely, inflated overrideRecord with three normal authoring decisions plus an undeclared build-tag scope extension, and omitted the EB-chat-handoff transition. This v1.0.1 amendment corrects content to canonical truth: closureStatus=closed-clean (patterns.yml landed in PR #311, no carry-forward debt); overrideRecord contains the single substrate-correction (patterns.yml authoring gap) plus the build-tag scope extension EB-chat self-disclosed in its apology post; rulePipelineEvidence captures the workflow-patterns-yml lesson; transitions correctly capture the EB-chat-handoff. The doctrine breach itself is captured as a separate governance deliverable: d-2026-06-06-sr-eb-chat-sr-repo-write-violation-incident.
closureNotes.overrideRecord:
Two in-execution corrections caught at EB CI before PR #311 merged:

1. patterns.yml authoring gap (substrate-correction). Spec authored 8 scope files; EB repo has audit-workflows.yml CI gate requiring every workflow file have a class+tier entry in infrastructure/workflows/patterns.yml. Both new workflows needed entries (build-dev-images.yml: {class: build-image, tier: develop}, deploy-dev.yml: {class: deploy-application, tier: develop}). L4 patched on feat branch at commit 9a2b389, CI re-ran green, then squash-merged. Authoring-layer failure: tarball authoring did not read audit-workflows.yml or patterns.yml at substrate review. EB-chat self-disclosed this in 2026-06-06 14:57 PDT apology post.

2. OUT-3 build tag scope extension (undeclared at authoring). Spec OUT-3 acceptance criteria stated 2-tag shape (:dev + :{full-sha}); EB-chat tarball authored 3-tag shape (:dev + :fullsha + :shortsha) for operational utility (shortsha enables DOCR GC retention matching). This was a scope extension beyond what the spec authorized and should have surfaced as a spec amendment at authoring time rather than being executed silently. EB-chat self-disclosed this in apology post; recorded here for the audit trail.

Forward rule (substrate-correction lesson, candidate for memory rule subject to fresh L4 named-step approval per memory #22): any future deliverable that adds workflow files MUST also patch infrastructure/workflows/patterns.yml with class+tier entries for each new workflow. Pre-authoring substrate read MUST include audit-workflows.yml + patterns.yml whenever .github/workflows/*.yml NEW files are in scope.
